articles.title: New GoGra malware for Linux uses Microsoft Graph API for comms
articles.summary: A Linux variant of the GoGra backdoor uses legitimate Microsoft infrastructure, relying on an Outlook inbox for stealthy payload delivery. [...]

articles.title: Google Antigravity in Crosshairs of Security Researchers, Cybercriminals
articles.summary: Researchers discovered a remote code execution vulnerability and cybercriminals are using its reputation to deliver malware. The post Google Antigravity in Crosshairs of Security Researchers, Cybercriminals appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
articles.summary: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges. articles.title: Oracle Patches 450 Vulnerabilities With April 2026 CPU
articles.summary: The company released 481 new security patches across 28 product families, including over 300 fixes for remotely exploitable, unauthenticated flaws. The post Oracle Patches 450 Vulnerabilities With April 2026 CPU appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: Microsoft has released out-of-band (OOB) security updates to patch a critical ASP.NET Core privilege escalation vulnerability. [...]

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new variant of a known malware called LOTUSLITE that's distributed via a theme related to India's banking sector. articles.title: Over 1,300 Microsoft SharePoint servers vulnerable to spoofing attacks
articles.summary: Over 1,300 Microsoft SharePoint servers exposed online remain unpatched against a spoofing vulnerability that was exploited as a zero-day and is still being abused in ongoing attacks. [...]

articles.title: French govt agency confirms breach as hacker offers to sell data
articles.summary: France Titres, the government agency in France for issuing and managing administrative documents has disclosed a data breach after a threat actor claimed the attack and stealing citizen data. [...]

articles.title: New Lotus data wiper used against Venezuelan energy, utility firms
articles.summary: A previously undocumented data-wiping malware dubbed Lotus was used last year in targeted attacks against energy and utilities organizations in Venezuela. [...]

articles.summary: Threat actors associated with The Gentlemen ransomware‑as‑a‑service (RaaS) operation have been observed attempting to deploy a known proxy malware called SystemBC. articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have identified 22 new vulnerabilities in popular models of serial-to-IP converters from Lantronix and Silex that could be exploited to hijack susceptible devices and tamper with data exchanged by them. articles.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
articles.summary: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. articles.title: Third US Security Expert Admits Helping Ransomware Gang
articles.summary: Angelo Martino of Florida has pleaded guilty to collaborating with the BlackCat cybercrime group while working as a ransomware negotiator. The post Third US Security Expert Admits Helping Ransomware Gang appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Aiding BlackCat Attacks in 2023
articles.summary: A third individual who was employed as a ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to conducting ransomware attacks against U.S. companies in 2023. articles.title: Cloud platform Vercel says company breached through third-party AI tool
articles.summary: Vercel released a statement acknowledging a breach and warning a “limited subset of customers” that their Vercel credentials were compromised.

articles.title: 5 Places where Mature SOCs Keep MTTR Fast and Others Waste Time
articles.summary: Security teams often present MTTR as an internal KPI. articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new iteration of an Android malware family called NGate that has been found to abuse a legitimate application called HandyPay instead of NFCGate. articles.title: Unsecured Perforce Servers Expose Sensitive Data From Major Orgs
articles.summary: Things are improving, but a researcher has still identified over 1,500 Perforce P4 instances allowing attackers to read files on the server. The post Unsecured Perforce Servers Expose Sensitive Data From Major Orgs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA flags new SD-WAN flaw as actively exploited in attacks

articles.title: Organizations Warned of Exploited Cisco, Kentico, Zimbra Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: CISA expanded the KEV catalog with eight flaws, but five of them have been flagged as exploited before. The post Organizations Warned of Exploited Cisco, Kentico, Zimbra Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Actively exploited Apache ActiveMQ flaw impacts 6,400 servers
articles.summary: Nonprofit security organization Shadowserver found that over 6,400 Apache ActiveMQ servers exposed online are vulnerable to ongoing attacks exploiting a high-severity code injection vulnerability. [...]

articles.summary: Grupo Seguritech is a Mexican surveillance company that is expanding into the US.

articles.title: Former ransomware negotiator pleads guilty to BlackCat attacks
articles.summary: 41-year-old Angelo Martino, a former employee of cybersecurity incident response company DigitalMint, has pleaded guilty to targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks in 2023. [...]

articles.title: $290 Million Kelp DAO Crypto Heist Blamed on North Korea
articles.summary: The hackers targeted LayerZero’s DVN, compromising certain RPCs and DDoSing others to trigger failover to the poisoned infrastructure. The post $290 Million Kelp DAO Crypto Heist Blamed on North Korea appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: A new variant of the NGate malware that steals NFC payment data is targeting Android users by hiding in a trojanized version of HandyPay, a legitimate mobile payments processing tool. [...]

articles.title: Ukraine busts ‘bot farm’ supplying thousands of fake Telegram accounts to Russian spies
articles.summary: Ukrainian authorities have dismantled a so-called “bot farm” that police say was supplying thousands of fake social media accounts to Russian intelligence services for use in disinformation campaigns against Ukraine.

articles.title: The Gentlemen ransomware now uses SystemBC for bot-powered attacks
articles.summary: A SystemBC proxy malware botnet of more than 1,570 hosts, believed to be corporate victims, has been discovered following an investigation into a Gentlemen ransomware attack carried out by a gang affiliate. [...]

articles.title: Seiko USA website defaced as hacker claims customer data theft
articles.summary: The Seiko USA website was defaced over the weekend, displaying a message from attackers claiming they stole its Shopify customer database and threatening to leak it unless a ransom is paid. [...]

articles.title: Crypto infrastructure company blames $290 million theft on North Korean hackers
articles.summary: A theft of nearly $300 million worth of cryptocurrency has been attributed to hackers from North Korea, as the industry grapples with the fallout of a wide-ranging incident involving multiple prominent platforms.

articles.title: Serial-to-IP Converter Flaws Expose OT and Healthcare Systems to Hacking
articles.summary: Forescout researchers discovered 20 new vulnerabilities in Lantronix and Silex products and described theoretical attack scenarios. The post Serial-to-IP Converter Flaws Expose OT and Healthcare Systems to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft: Teams increasingly abused in helpdesk impersonation attacks
articles.summary: Microsoft is warning of threat actors increasingly abusing external Microsoft Teams collaboration and relying on legitimate tools for access and lateral movement on enterprise networks. [...]

articles.title: Cyberattack at French identity document agency may have exposed personal data
articles.summary: A cyberattack targeting a French government website used to manage identity documents and driver’s licenses may have exposed users’ personal data, the Interior Ministry said.
